vocab.meaning: bắt đầu nhú lên, nảy mầm
vocab.example: I remember the excitement of seeing the first tiny green leaves sprout up from the soil after weeks of waiting.
When I was little, I used to love to dig up a small patch of the garden to plant potatoes with my father.
In the height of summer, we had to water down the flower beds every evening to keep the lilies from wilting.

vocab.meaning: phát triển mạnh mẽ nhờ vào (điều kiện cụ thể)
vocab.example: The ferns I kept in the shade seemed to thrive on the damp atmosphere of our back porch.

vocab.meaning: vứt bỏ, loại bỏ (những cây đã chết hoặc hỏng)
vocab.example: It was always a bit sad when we had to toss out the withered marigolds at the end of the autumn season.

vocab.meaning: mang vào trong nhà (để tránh thời tiết xấu)
vocab.example: My mother would remind me to bring in the potted herbs whenever a storm was brewing.

vocab.meaning: khô cạn, mất nước hoàn toàn
vocab.example: I quickly learned that if the soil were to dry out completely, the delicate seedlings wouldn't survive the night.

vocab.meaning: xuất hiện bất ngờ (thường là cỏ dại hoặc hoa dại)
vocab.example: Wildflowers would often pop up in the middle of our vegetable patch, adding a splash of unexpected color.

vocab.meaning: buộc cố định (thân cây vào cọc)
vocab.example: We had to tie up the beanstalks to bamboo poles to help them climb as they grew taller.
It was my daily responsibility to look after the jasmine bush near our front gate.
